replies.msg:
補充

人手去brute force iPhone passcode 問題係錯太多次系統會限制你retry 既時間，例如要再等幾百甚至幾千分鐘
但有咗Cellebrite 可以避開呢個限制
Tools like those from Cellebrite and Grayshift don’t actually break iPhones’ encryption; they guess the password. To do so, they exploit flaws in the software, like Checkm8, to remove the limit of 10 password attempts. (After about 10 failed attempts, an iPhone erases its data.) The tools then use a so-called brute-force attack, which automatically tries thousands of passcodes until one works.

That approach means the wild card in the Pensacola case is the length of the suspect’s passcode. If it’s six numbers — the default on iPhones — authorities almost certainly can break it. If it’s longer, it might be impossible.

A four-number passcode, the previous default length, would take on average about seven minutes to guess. If it’s six digits, it would take on average about 11 hours. Eight digits: 46 days. Ten digits: 12.5 years.

If the passcode uses both numbers and letters, there are far more possible passcodes — and thus cracking it takes much longer. A six-character alphanumeric passcode would take on average 72 years to guess.


https://daringfireball.net/linked/2020/01/20/how-modern-iphone-encryption-works

quote.msg: 學到野

補充

人手去brute force iPhone passcode 問題係錯太多次系統會限制你retry 既時間，例如要再等幾百甚至幾千分鐘
但有咗Cellebrite 可以避開呢個限制
Tools like those from Cellebrite and Grayshift don’t actually break iPhones’ encryption; they guess the password. To do so, they exploit flaws in the software, like Checkm8, to remove the limit of 10 password attempts. (After about 10 failed attempts, an iPhone erases its data.) The tools then use a so-called brute-force attack, which automatically tries thousands of passcodes until one works.

That approach means the wild card in the Pensacola case is the length of the suspect’s passcode. If it’s six numbers — the default on iPhones — authorities almost certainly can break it. If it’s longer, it might be impossible.

A four-number passcode, the previous default length, would take on average about seven minutes to guess. If it’s six digits, it would take on average about 11 hours. Eight digits: 46 days. Ten digits: 12.5 years.

If the passcode uses both numbers and letters, there are far more possible passcodes — and thus cracking it takes much longer. A six-character alphanumeric passcode would take on average 72 years to guess.


https://daringfireball.net/linked/2020/01/20/how-modern-iphone-encryption-works

replies.msg:
留意翻extraction同bypass lock係兩回事，你見cellebrite嘅supported devices度 extraction supported list幾乎乜柒機都有 佢開左你部機咪可以extract你，唔支援extraction嘅意思只係佢無得1click完美複製你資料，要手動提取你啲files同擷圖，老實講如果去到呢部，你肯定可以洗定8月15。舉例cellebrite最近大大聲話自己extract 到signal跟住比signal兜巴升到佢收翻埋個blog

https://www.forensicfocus.com/forums/mobile-forensics/samsung-secure-folder-decrypt/
第一個reply係另一間公司嘅銷售代表，佢話回覆當時應該未有方法可以解鎖secure folder （無記得2018年好似有個hacker爆到knox跟住report比三叔，修復之後就再揾唔到knox bypass呢樣嘢)。 只要你唔好開secure folder嘅cloud backup就得。 新版secure folder無記錯好似有個選項比你選擇唔準透過三叔ac recover密碼，開埋就無死

舊版係如果手機爆但未爆secure folder，有機會透過你已經登入左嘅三叔ac + sim卡+你裝係knox出面嘅email app去重設你三叔ac密碼，跟住再係knox登入度揀重設knox密碼

replies.msg:
其實安全有好多種, 例如唔會上下網就比人hijack咗部機被種木馬係一種
但呢個post 講嘅安全係指部電話被第三方扣押期間嘅資料安全

唔需要深究佢用乜嘢手段去防守，衹要知結果
而Cellebrite 就係呢方面嘅天書 (同埋樓主篇新聞嘅結果)
https://www.cellebrite.com/en/cas-supported-devices/

File-Based Encrypted (FBE) Android full file system extraction (Samsung / Huawei / Google Pixel / LG / Motorola, etc.)

• Samsung Galaxy S10, Note 10, and all A Series (2019) brute force passcode
• Huawei P30, P20, Mate 20 and many others (2017 and newer) brute force passcode
• Qualcomm and MediaTek based devices brute force passcode
• After-First-Unlock (AFU) extraction (must keep device alive seizure!), including Google Pixel 2/3/4


Full-Disk Encryption (FDE) Android physical extraction (Samsung, Huawei, Google, LG, Lenovo, Motorola, Nokia, Sony, etc.)

• Brute force Secure Startup passcodes
• Bypass, brute force, or disable screen lock passcode


Android Encrypted Container extraction (find evidence you may have overlooked)

• Samsung Secure Folder (knox folder)
• Huawei PrivateSpace
• Xiaomi Second Space support coming soon


你可以話小米暫時安全, 因為破解佢嘅piorirty 唔夠高
priority*
